goal: Apply PhysicsCollisionAPI to 5 pre-seeded cube meshes at /World/Box_0..4 in a single bulk_apply_schema call. Verify one via list_applied_schemas.
thoughts: bulk_apply_schema is the atomic batch primitive — one RPC round-trip vs five. The schema id must be the fully qualified PhysicsCollisionAPI (without 'Physics' prefix it's a silent no-op). CollisionAPI is meaningful on Mesh/primitive-shape prims; Xform parents get the schema but do nothing useful.
code:
# Single bulk call
paths = [f"/World/Box_{i}" for i in range(5)]
bulk_apply_schema(
    prim_paths=paths,
    api_schema="PhysicsCollisionAPI"
)

# Spot-verify one
list_applied_schemas(prim_path="/World/Box_0")
failure_modes: Looping apply_api_schema five times — correct but violates the batch-tool principle and is 5x the RPC cost.
Passing api_schema='CollisionAPI' without the Physics prefix — silent no-op on wrong schema name.
Applying to an Xform parent thinking it propagates — schemas don't propagate to descendants.
